text: In agile methodology, work is broken into small, manageable phases called sprints, which usually last one to four weeks. Each sprint focuses on completing specific tasks from a project backlog. These tasks are often described as user stories—simple explanations of a feature from the end user’s perspective. Team members meet briefly each day for standups, where they share progress, plans, and any obstacles. This approach supports iterative development, meaning the team continuously improves the product after each cycle. Agile encourages flexibility and regular feedback, allowing teams to adapt quickly to changes and deliver value step by step.
